payload.description: Main Line concrete tie installation, switch installation near Floral Park, and rail replacement between Hicksville and Bethpage

evidence_refs.source_quote: weekend of September 16-17

payload.claim_text: Service improvements include more through service to Atlantic Terminal in the AM peak with one additional AM peak service opportunity, more express service between Jamaica and Penn Station, an added service opportunity from Jamaica to Penn Station in the peak of the AM peak to alleviate crowding, more AM peak express service on the Huntington and Ronkonkoma Branches, one additional AM peak train originating in Hicksville, one additional AM peak train originating in Westbury, and one additional shuttle train from Atlantic Terminal to Jamaica in the 3pm-4pm hour

Service improvements include two Ronkonkoma Branch trains diverted from Grand Central Madison to Penn Station in the 4pm-5pm hour, one Ronkonkoma Branch train changing from early release days only to Monday through Friday operation, three PM peak Ronkonkoma trains making additional stops east of Brentwood, more service opportunities from Penn Station following concerts and sporting events at Madison Square Garden, and in off-peak periods select trains west of Jamaica making stops at both Kew Gardens and Forest Hills rather than the skip-stop pattern

Service adjustments within guidelines: one lightly-patronized AM peak Ronkonkoma Branch train eliminated, one lightly-patronized AM peak Babylon Branch train eliminated, one lightly-patronized PM peak Port Washington Branch train eliminated

Construction activities: Grand Central Madison contractor maintenance on weekday and weekend overnights between Grand Central Madison and Plaza Interlocking, with overnight trains on Port Washington, Long Beach and Far Rockaway Branches diverted to Penn Station; FRA Inspections and Maintenance on Atlantic Branch between Atlantic Terminal and Dunton Interlocking on midday/weekdays, with eastbound and westbound service between Jamaica and Atlantic Terminal reduced to hourly for one hour following the AM peak period

Trackwork program diversions: On August 12-13 and August 19-20 weekends, Grand Central Madison contractor maintenance requires Hempstead Branch trains to terminate/originate at Atlantic Terminal, West Hempstead Branch trains to terminate/originate in Jamaica, all Port Washington Branch trains to divert to Penn Station, and all Huntington Branch trains to divert to Penn Station. On August 26-27, Van Wyck Bridge waterproofing requires West Hempstead and Babylon Branch trains to operate on adjusted schedules. On September 16-17, Main Line concrete tie and switch installation requires service adjustments on Huntington, Ronkonkoma, and Oyster Bay Branches.
Funding for these projects is contained in the Long Island Rail Road Operating and MTA Capital Program budget.
